| **Significant** | Special Committee includes interested director (Okafor), undermining committee independence | XI.A |
| **Significant** | RRA termination hinges on undefined "Deemed Liquidation Event" — post-closing survival risk | VIII.B, XII |
| **Significant** | Document gaps: 2021 Equity Incentive Plan, award agreements, stockholder consent records, possible separate Investors' Rights / ROFR-Co-Sale agreements | VIII.C, IX.B |
| **Significant** | Equity plan share-reserve increase (8.0M → 9.5M) — Board approval only; stockholder approval status unknown | IX.D, X.A |
| **Moderate** | Cross-document definitional inconsistencies (50% vs. majority; Liquidation Event vs. Sale of the Company vs. Deemed Liquidation Event) | VIII.A |
| **Moderate** | ROFR/co-sale carve-out conditioned on Sale "consummated in accordance with Article 3" (drag-along) | VI.B |
| **Moderate** | Stonemill board observer absent from April 17, 2024 meeting — potential observer-right breach | XI.B |
| **Moderate** | Voting Agreement 5-year sunset (Nov. 3, 2026) — lingering risk if Sale of the Company not cleanly triggered | VII.D |
| **Moderate** | Outstanding employee options appear out-of-the-money ($14.30 exercise price vs. ~$13.95 implied per-share value) | IX.C |

---

## Appendix A — Consent / Approval Matrix

| # | Approval / Consent | Source Provision | Threshold | Parties Whose Consent Is Needed | Current Status |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Board approval of definitive merger agreement | DGCL § 251(b); Bylaws § 3.7 | Majority of directors present at a quorum (≥4 of 7) | Board of Directors | Not yet obtained (definitive docs pending); negotiation authorized 6-1 on Sept. 25, 2024 |
| 2 | Stockholder approval (merger) | DGCL § 251(c); Restated Certificate Art. VI | Majority of outstanding shares entitled to vote, as-converted, single class (>16,950,000 of 33,900,000) | All stockholders (Common + Preferred as-converted) | Not yet obtained; may be taken by written consent (DGCL § 228) |
| 3 | All-Preferred protective provision (Liquidation Event) | Restated Certificate § 4.3.6(a)(vi) | Majority (>50%) of Preferred, as-converted, single class (>8,250,000 of 16,500,000) | Preferred holders (Ridgeline, Northbridge, Stonemill) | Not yet obtained; **achievable without Stonemill** (Ridgeline + Northbridge = 8,700,000) |
| 4 | Series C separate-class protective provision (Liquidation Event) | Restated Certificate § 4.3.6(b)(i) | 66⅔% of Series C, separate class (≥5,200,000 of 7,800,000) | Series C holders (Stonemill = 100%) | Not yet obtained; **CANNOT be obtained without Stonemill — HARD BLOCK** |
| 5 | Drag-along trigger (if invoked) | Stockholders' Agreement § 3.1(a) | (i) Common majority as-converted (>16,950,000); (ii) Preferred majority as-converted (>8,250,000); (iii) 2 of 3 Investors | All Restricted Stockholders (to be compelled) | Not yet invoked; (ii) and (iii) met without Stonemill; (i) short by ~950,000 shares (needs option/common-holder support) |
| 6 | Drag-along override of Series C veto | Stockholders' Agreement § 3.3 (proxy) + Restated Certificate § 4.3.6(b)(i) | Compelled Series C vote satisfying 66⅔% | N/A (enforcement question) | **Legally uncertain** — requires Delaware-law analysis; likely contested |
| 7 | Stockholders' Agreement termination | Stockholders' Agreement § 6.1(b) | Sale of the Company (automatic) | N/A | Will terminate at closing (merger = Sale of the Company) |
| 8 | Voting Agreement termination | Voting Agreement § 7.1(b) | Sale of the Company (automatic) | N/A | Will terminate at closing (merger = Sale of the Company); sunset Nov. 3, 2026 if not triggered |
| 9 | RRA termination | RRA § 9(c) | "Deemed Liquidation Event" (undefined) | N/A (automatic) or written consent (Company + majority of Registrable Securities) | **Ambiguous** (undefined term); recommend express termination at closing |
| 10 | ROFR / co-sale waiver (belt-and-suspenders) | Stockholders' Agreement §§ 2.1(c), 2.7(e) | Sale "consummated in accordance with Article 3" or written waivers | Restricted Stockholders | Not yet obtained; structure to invoke drag-along and/or obtain waivers |
| 11 | Clearfield debt repayment / lender consent | Loan agreement (not yet reviewed) | Per loan agreement change-of-control provisions | Clearfield National Bank | Not yet confirmed; obtain and review loan agreement |
| 12 | Equity award treatment / acceleration | 2021 Equity Incentive Plan (not yet received) | Per Plan change-of-control / acceleration provisions | Plan administrator / award holders | Unknown — Plan and award agreements outstanding |

---

## Appendix B — Document Request List

The following documents should be requested from Avery, Pollard & Glenn (Nathaniel Rourke), routed through Jonathan Hsieh. We should not communicate directly with Company counsel or representatives.

1. 2021 Equity Incentive Plan, as amended (including the July 2023 amendment increasing the share reserve from 8,000,000 to 9,500,000 shares).
2. All individual equity award agreements (stock option agreements, RSU agreements) granted under the Plan, including the Raj Malhotra option agreement (350,000 options, $14.30 exercise price) and the Linda Yeung RSU agreement (200,000 RSUs).
3. Current capitalization table and schedule of outstanding equity awards, with vesting schedules, exercise prices, and grant dates.
4. Stockholder written-consent records for the period January 2023 through September 2024, including any consents relating to: (a) the Clearfield National Bank term loan (October 2023); (b) the 2021 Equity Incentive Plan share-reserve increase (July 2023); and (c) any other actions implicating the Restated Certificate protective provisions (Section 4.3.6).
5. Confirmation of the complete inventory of governance agreements executed as of November 3, 2021 — specifically, whether separate "Investors' Rights Agreement" and "Right of First Refusal and Co-Sale Agreement" exist as standalone documents (as referenced in the Board Minutes), or whether the Stockholders' Agreement and Registration Rights Agreement are the operative combined instruments. If separate agreements exist, complete copies.
6. SensorLink Technologies LOI (executed March 8, 2024) and any related correspondence, including any written termination notice delivered to SensorLink.
7. Clearfield National Bank term loan agreement, promissory note, security agreement, intellectual property security agreement, and all ancillary loan documents.
8. Confidentiality agreement(s) executed with Hargrove Capital Partners III, LP, and any exclusivity agreement with Hargrove.
9. Meridian Partners LLC engagement letter and any process materials / indication-of-interest correspondence.
10. Thornbury & Associates engagement letter and any draft or final fairness opinion / valuation analysis.
11. Indemnification agreements between the Company and each director (referenced in Bylaws Section 6.4).
12. Joinder agreements executed by any common stockholders or option holders under the Stockholders' Agreement and/or Voting Agreement.
13. The Company's most recent 409A valuation report(s) supporting the $14.30 fair market value determination.
14. Any stockholder notices, objection letters, or written communications from Stonemill or Julian Trask (beyond the April 19, 2024 email already produced) relating to the strategic alternatives process or the Hargrove Transaction.

---

## Appendix C — Capitalization and Waterfall Summary

**Authorized capital (Restated Certificate § 4.1):** 72,000,000 shares — 50,000,000 Common ($0.001 par); 22,000,000 Preferred ($0.001 par), designated as 5,000,000 Series A, 7,000,000 Series B, and 10,000,000 Series C.

**Outstanding (per Stockholders' Agreement Exhibit B and Board Minutes):**

| Class | Shares | OIP/Share | Liquidation Preference |
|---|---|---|---|
| Series C Preferred (Stonemill) | 7,800,000 | $18.50 | $144,300,000 |
| Series B Preferred (Northbridge) | 5,200,000 | $9.25 | $48,100,000 |
| Series A Preferred (Ridgeline) | 3,500,000 | $4.00 | $14,000,000 |
| Common — Founders (Ellery + Chandrasekaran) | 7,300,000 | n/a | — |
| Common / Options / RSUs (other) | 10,100,000 | n/a | — |
| **Total fully diluted** | **33,900,000** | | **$206,400,000** |

**Waterfall (non-participating Preferred, § 4.3.2):** Series C → Series B → Series A → Common. Because implied equity value ($473M) exceeds aggregate preference ($206.4M), all Preferred is in the money on an as-converted basis; the waterfall is expected to collapse to an as-converted distribution. Implied blended per-share value: ~$13.95 ($473M ÷ 33.9M), subject to treasury-stock-method adjustments for options and the precise waterfall.

**Anti-dilution:** Broad-based weighted average only (no full ratchet) (§ 4.3.4). Conversion ratio 1:1 initial (§ 4.3.3). Mandatory conversion on IPO ≥ $75M proceeds at ≥ $55.50/share, or 60% Preferred consent (§ 4.3.3(b)).

---

*This memorandum is attorney work product prepared in anticipation of litigation and is privileged and confidential. It is prepared for the internal use of Whitfield & Crane LLP and its client, Hargrove Capital Management, LLC, in connection with the proposed acquisition of Terravox Systems, Inc. It should not be disclosed to any third party (including the Company, its counsel, or its stockholders) without the prior consent of Jonathan Hsieh or Elena Marchetti. The analysis herein is based on the governance documents made available as of the date hereof and is subject to revision as additional documents (identified in Appendix B) are received and reviewed.*"}

text:
The governance diligence memo is complete. Here is a summary of what I produced.

## Deliverable

**`output/governance-diligence-memo.docx`** — a comprehensive, privileged attorney-work-product governance diligence memorandum (~12,100 words, ~22 pages with tables and appendices) addressed to Elena Marchetti and Jonathan Hsieh at Whitfield & Crane LLP, covering the proposed acquisition of Terravox Systems, Inc. by Hargrove Capital Partners III, LP.

## How I approached it

I read all seven governance documents (Restated Certificate, Bylaws, Stockholders' Agreement, Voting Agreement, Registration Rights Agreement, Board Minutes, and the Hsieh scope email) and cross-referenced their provisions against the ten diligence areas and the client's overarching concern about Stonemill's leverage.

## Key findings the memo develops

- **Critical — Stonemill's hard blocking right.** As holder of 100% of the Series C Preferred, Stonemill controls the § 4.3.6(b)(i) separate-class protective provision (66⅔% of Series C required to effect any Liquidation Event). The reverse triangular merger is unambiguously a Liquidation Event, so the deal cannot close without Stonemill's affirmative consent.
- **Critical / time-sensitive — SensorLink LOI.** The LOI's exclusivity auto-renews in 90-day increments unless a written termination notice was sent before Sept. 8, 2024; the Minutes reflect uncertainty whether notice was sent. A live exclusivity could conflict with signing the Hargrove merger agreement.
- **Significant — drag-along vs. charter protective provision.** I ran the numbers (the drag-along's Preferred-majority and 2-of-3-investor prongs can be met without Stonemill; the Common-majority prong is short by ~950,000 shares) and analyzed the unsettled Delaware-law question of whether the drag-along can compel a Series C vote that *satisfies* the charter veto — recommending it as leverage/fallback, not a clean path.
- **Significant** items on the $12M Clearfield term loan (apparent § 4.3.6(a)(v) protective-provision violation), Special Committee independence (Okafor is Northbridge's interested designee), the undefined "Deemed Liquidation Event" in the RRA, the equity-plan share-reserve increase, and document gaps (Plan, award agreements, possible separate Investors' Rights/ROFR-Co-Sale agreements).

## Structure

Executive Summary → Transaction Overview/Capitalization → ten numbered sections (one per diligence area) → consolidated Stonemill leverage map → prioritized next steps/action items → severity classification → and three appendices (Consent/Approval Matrix, Document Request List, Capitalization/Waterfall Summary). The memo is marked privileged/work product throughout.

The .docx was generated via Pandoc and passed the docx validation gate (ZIP integrity, XML well-formedness, content-type and relationship consistency).
